subject: Cloud Computing
title: Caching Strategies
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Caching Strategies.

hook.text: Your database handles 10,000 queries per second. A cache in front of it reduces that to 500. Same results, less strain.

buildup.text: A cache stores frequently accessed data in fast memory. Redis and Memcached are the most popular cloud caches.

discovery.text: Cache-aside, write-through, and write-behind are common patterns. Each balances speed, freshness, and complexity.

twist.text: Cache invalidation is famously one of the hardest problems in computing. Stale data causes subtle, nasty bugs.

climax.text: A well-designed cache can make an application feel 10-100x faster. A poorly designed one creates data nightmares.

punchline.text: Caching is easy to start. Cache invalidation is hard to master.

quiz.question: What makes cache invalidation so difficult?
options: Determining when cached data is stale and serving outdated data causes subtle bugs
Caches can only store data for a maximum of 5 minutes
Cloud providers charge extra for cache invalidation requests
